source_jobs.title: Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
PRODUCT ENGINEER




ABOUT GAIA

Mission: A world where anyone who wants a family can try.

We're transforming fertility care from the ground up to make it more accessible. At Gaia, we believe that when it comes to starting a family, financial barriers shouldn't determine who gets to try. We're building the technology infrastructure that makes fertility treatments available to everyone, not just the fortunate few who can afford a $50k bill upfront.




THE CHALLENGE WE'RE SOLVING

Fertility affects 1 in 6 people, yet only 2% of babies are born via IVF. We're changing that by making fertility care 10x better, with 2x better outcomes, at half the cost.

Our founder experienced this firsthand - going through IVF and having a child because he could afford a $70k bill. Most people can't. We're here to change that reality.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD

You will not be just building software - you will be architecting the infrastructure that makes family building accessible to everyone. This is where fintech meets healthcare in the most meaningful way possible.

🎯 The Gaia Orchestrator: Our internal framework that automates care navigation, financing, clinic operations, and risk tracking across a complex ecosystem of patients, clinics, lenders, and insurers. You'll own real slices of this - state machines, workflows, and integrations with external systems that weren't designed to talk to each other.

🧠 AI-Native Operations Layer: We use AI not as a feature but as infrastructure. Our ops, compliance, and member workflows run on LLM-powered agents and scheduled routines. You'll build and extend these - not just consume them.

⚡ Real-time Underwriting Engine: Predictive modeling in a risk layer no one else has touched, adapting to patient and clinical data to make fertility care financially accessible. You'll work on the models and the pipelines that feed them.

💙 Member-Facing Experiences: Crafting the moments that matter most - from pre-cycle intake to outcome reporting. People come to Gaia at one of the most vulnerable points in their lives. The experiences you build will shape how that feels.




THE TECHNICAL REALITY

We're building in a space that's:

🔒 Highly regulated: Healthcare and finance bring an interesting combination of complex regulatory and safety concerns. HIPAA, SOC 2 Type II, and PHI are first-class constraints, not afterthoughts. If you've built in regulated environments, you'll hit the ground running.

❤️ Emotionally charged: The people using what you build are going through a complex and emotionally charged journey of building a family. Every interaction, every piece of data, every experience you ship exists in that context. That weight is real, and it makes the craft matter more.

💰 Financially complex: We sit at the intersection of lending, insurance, and outcome-based pricing in a domain where the numbers are high and the stakes are personal. The models are non-trivial and the edge cases are endless. You'll need to think beyond the happy path.

🔄 Technically fragmented: the healthcare industry is fragmented with complex technologies and protocols. These systems were not meant to talk to each other. You'll build integrations that are robust enough to handle the messiness of the real world, and resilient enough not to break when it does.

Your solutions will directly impact people's ability to start families. Everything you build will touch lives directly.




YOU'LL THRIVE HERE IF YOU'RE

🚀 Unreasonable - You believe impossible problems deserve creative solutions

🦁 Brave - You're comfortable building in ambiguity and changing the status quo

🔍 Accountable - You take ownership of problems that span across complex domains and technology

🔄 Adaptable - You can pivot quickly in a fast-moving environment

💗 Kind - You care deeply about the people depending on what you build




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Experience: 5+ years as a full-stack engineer in startup environments

Technical Skills:

 - Strong Python and TypeScript.

source_jobs.title: Product Lead, Consumer Health
source_jobs.description:
At Gaia Family https://gaiafamily.com/?utm_source=chatgpt.com, we're building the patient-facing front-door to fertility care, starting with financial infrastructure to pay monthly with outcomes protection baked in.

Most people pursuing IVF or egg freezing in the U.S. are paying out of pocket. The experience is expensive, fragmented, emotionally overwhelming, and operationally messy. We partner with 180+ leading fertility clinics to make treatment more accessible through financing, product innovation, and a modern patient experience.

We’re an ambitious, fast-moving team backed by top-tier investors (including Valar Ventures and Atomico), and we’re looking for a product leader to own and scale our consumer growth engine.




THE ROLE

This is not a feature PM role.

You will own Gaia’s consumer funnel end-to-end: from first touch through consult booking, financing, and treatment activation. You’ll be responsible for improving conversion, increasing revenue, and helping thousands of patients navigate one of the biggest healthcare decisions of their lives.

You’ll work directly with the Head of Product, alongside engineering, design, marketing, operations, and clinic partners. You’ll have high autonomy, fast shipping velocity, and direct influence on company strategy.

If you like operating close to the business, running experiments, obsessing over conversion, and building products that meaningfully impact people’s lives, you’ll thrive here.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - The full consumer journey:
   
   - acquisition → signup → consult → financing → treatment

 - Core growth and conversion metrics tied directly to company revenue

 - Patient-facing experiences across:
   
   - onboarding
   
   - scheduling
   
   - intake flows
   
   - SMS + email lifecycle journeys
   
   - payment and financing UX

 - Experimentation strategy:
   
   - identifying opportunities
   
   - designing tests
   
   - analyzing outcomes
   
   - deciding what scales

 - New consumer products and monetization opportunities, including lab testing and adjacent offerings

 - Funnel analytics and reporting:
   
   - dashboards
   
   - conversion tracking
   
   - cohort analysis
   
   - operational visibility

 - Cross-functional execution with engineering, growth, operations, and fertility clinic partners




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR




MUST-HAVES

 - 5–8 years of product management experience

 - Experience owning growth, conversion, marketplace, or consumer funnels

 - Proven track record improving key business metrics through experimentation

 - Comfortable owning revenue outcomes, not just shipping features

 - Strong product intuition paired with analytical rigor

 - Highly autonomous operator who thrives in ambiguity

 - Experience in fast-moving startup environments (Series A/B preferred)

 - NYC-based and excited to work in person several days per week


STRONG PLUSES

 - Consumer health, fintech, marketplace, or healthcare experience

 - Experience with high-consideration or emotionally sensitive purchases

 - Women’s health or fertility experience

 - Experience building communication-heavy workflows (SMS, lifecycle, scheduling, onboarding)

 - Comfort using AI tools in day-to-day product work:
   
   - research
   
   - prototyping
   
   - analysis
   
   - writing
   
   - execution




HOW WE WORK

We move fast, operate with high ownership, and care deeply about quality.

We believe great product teams combine strong judgment with fast iteration. We run lots of experiments, stay close to customers, and prioritize business impact over process theater.

We’re also highly AI-native. We use AI throughout product development, operations, analysis, and communication. The people who succeed here tend to be naturally curious builders who adopt new tools quickly and constantly look for leverage.




WHY THIS ROLE IS EXCITING 🤩

 - Own a critical revenue-driving product surface end-to-end

 - Work on a meaningful problem in healthcare with massive mar

source_jobs.title: Product Lead, Employer
source_jobs.description:
At Gaia Family https://gaiafamily.com/?utm_source=chatgpt.com, we're building modern fertility coverage that actually works — for patients and for employers. We partner with companies to offer IVF and egg freezing as a structured, outcomes-aligned, benefit that complements our existing consumer offering.

We’ve already signed our first employer logos but are still early. We’re hiring a Product Manager to own and build our Employer product end-to-end. This is a zero-to-one GM role disguised as a PM role.

We’re an ambitious, fast-moving team backed by top-tier investors (including Valar Ventures and Atomico).




THE ROLE

You will own the Employer product line like a business, in close partnership with our Head of Employer sales. You will:

 - Define the strategy

 - Ship the product

 - Close feedback loops with customers (both employers & employees)

 - Drive revenue impact

 - Build the system from scratch

You’ll also work directly with our Head of Product, engineering team, exec team, Ops, Finance and Legal.

If you want to just manage a backlog, this is not the job for you. If you want to build a business inside a company, it is 💪




WHAT YOU WILL OWN:

1. Employer Product Strategy

You will define the long-term vision for Gaia’s employer offering, turning ambiguity into a crisp roadmap. This will require you to clarify ICPs (mid-market vs enterprise) alongside Sales, whilst working with Finance to shape packaging, pricing, and plan design.



2. End-to-End Employer and Employee Experience

We want to create a lovable employer and employee experience that stands out in the fertility & family-building landscape. 🧡

You’ll be responsible for surface areas ranging from customer sales conversations to the implementation & onboarding experience, and employee enrollment & usage.

You will need to obsess over the full system, not just one aspect.



3. Revenue & Unit Economics

You will co-leading the ROI strategy for our employer product. This means:

 - Understanding case rates, utilization, loss ratios

 - Partnering with Finance on pricing experiments

 - Improving margins through product & process design

You will focus on these metrics like a GM would.



4. Cross-Functional Collaboration

You will work with teams across the business, you will:

 - Align with Sales & Legal on what we sell

 - Partner with Ops on what we can operationalize

 - Work with Eng to build scalable systems

 - Run tight weekly reviews




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Someone who...

 - Is deeply collaborative with a builder mindset

 - Is solution-oriented - when we encounter challenges, you are already brainstorming on how to solve

 - Is curious and eager to learn

 - Has deep ownership

 - Works cross-functionally successfully

 - Isn't afraid to roll up their sleeves and when needed, "wear many hats" to execute successfully

 - Is AI-native and excited about the future of EPD & tech




YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU…

 - Have 5–10 years B2B experience in product or growth roles - ideally in high-growth fast-paced startups

 - Have built or worked on an employer healthcare product before

 - Care about revenue and margin, not just UX polish

 - Love ambiguity

 - Are AI-native, constantly testing out the latest tools to scale yourself & your team

 - Can go from whiteboard → SQL → customer call → Figma → pricing model in the same week

 - Have high energy and bias toward action

Bonus if:

 - You’re an ex founder

 - You’ve owned revenue targets before

 - Have experience in fertility, family-building, or HRA administration




HOW WE WORK

We move fast, operate with high ownership, and care deeply about quality.

We believe great product teams combine strong judgment with fast iteration. We run lots of experiments, stay close to customers, and prioritize business impact over process theater.

We’re also highly AI-native. We use AI throughout product development, operations, analysis, and communication. The people who succeed here tend to be naturally

source_jobs.title: Financial Controller
source_jobs.description:
FINANCIAL CONTROLLER




THE ROLE

Gaia is looking for an experienced, highly technical Controller to own and strengthen our core accounting and financial operations as we continue to scale as a US-based company. This is a critical leadership role for someone who is hands-on, detail-oriented, and brings strong technical accounting judgment in a fast-paced startup environment.

The Controller will report to the CFO and will be responsible for the integrity of our financials, the monthly close process, audit readiness, and building scalable accounting processes across the business. This role will partner closely with cross-functional leaders and play a key role in supporting investor reporting, decision-making, and compliance as we grow.

This is a US-based role.




WHO YOU ARE

You are a CPA with deep technical accounting expertise and high standards for accuracy and quality. You take ownership of your work end to end and are comfortable rolling up your sleeves in a lean environment. You are thoughtful about process, understand the “why” behind accounting decisions, and can balance technical rigor with business pragmatism.

You thrive in ambiguity, enjoy building and improving systems, and are confident pushing back when something is not right. You are organized, deadline-driven, and comfortable working closely with leadership in a high-growth company.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Own the monthly close process end to end, ensuring timely, accurate financial statements

 - Maintain the integrity of the general ledger, including reconciliations, journal entries, and accounting policies

 - Lead preparation for annual audits and manage relationships with external auditors and tax advisors

 - Ensure compliance with US GAAP and maintain strong internal controls

 - Oversee accounts payable, accounts receivable, payroll accounting, and expense management

 - Review and analyze monthly operating expenses and budget variances, identifying trends, risks, and areas for improvement

 - Partner with Finance leadership on financial reporting, including monthly and quarterly investor reporting

 - Own intercompany accounting and reconciliations, as applicable

 - Support tax compliance and filings in partnership with external advisors

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Operations, Product, Tech, People, and other teams to ensure financial accuracy and alignment

 - Identify, design, and implement process improvements to increase efficiency, accuracy, and scalability

 - Help transition and stabilize accounting operations from existing contractors and legacy processes


WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

 - Active CPA required

 - 7+ years of progressive accounting experience, including Controller or Assistant Controller experience

 - Deep technical accounting expertise and strong knowledge of US GAAP

 - Proven experience owning the close process and audit management

 - High attention to detail with a strong sense of accountability

 - Ability to operate both strategically and hands-on in a startup or high-growth environment

 - Strong analytical skills with the ability to communicate complex financial information clearly

 - Experience working with modern accounting systems (Xero experience a plus)

 - Experience with multi-entity or group accounting and consolidations

 - Exposure to fintech, lending, insurance, or regulated financial products is a strong plus


COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS

The base salary range for this role is $140,000–$180,000, depending on experience and alignment with the role.

At Gaia, our people are the foundation of our success. We are committed to supporting our team’s wellbeing, growth, and long-term potential.

In addition to a competitive, market-benchmarked salary, we offer:

 - Comprehensive healthcare coverage

 - Fertility and family-building support

 - A home office or setup budget for new hires

 - An annual personal development budget

 - Ongoing work on reward and recognition programs to ensure exceptional p

notes: Gaia Family is a fertility care startup with a strong focus on product development and innovation.
